ASTRO BUSINESS SOFTWARE

Knowledge base and manual


Preface

 

If you want to learn how to use the software and occasionally want to find some information or find a solution to a problem, this Knowledge Base * handbook will be helpful, written - and still being written, as the software is about. - for average users to understand.

* The Knowledge Base helps you to quickly find meaningful, accurate information by linking it to links and having good searchability. It also helps to solve problems. In this case, a user-friendly, knowledge-based manual will help you to gain continuous point-to-point knowledge.

Learning how to use the software is also helped by location-sensitive help: if you use the mouse pointer over an icon, button, switch, or text input field for two seconds, a bubble appears with information and information about that feature.

 

Information, help request

 

If you don't find enough information about something or if you have any other questions or requests about the software, or you may have been stuck, please contact our online customer service at:

·        E-mail: astro.central@europe.com

·        Skype: Astro Business Software Helpdesk (astro.helpdesk)
  On Skype, we are also looking forward to writing your questions, where we will answer your questions immediately. Click here to download Skype.
 

·        If you use our maintenance and repair service, you must download and install a desktop sharing and remote control software on your computer.
If you already have such remote administration software on your computer, you can send the ID and the password and detailed description of the problem via our email or Skype contact information.
At the agreed time, we enter and carry out repairs, maintenance, and follow up on your own screen.

For quality assurance reasons, we prefer online written contact with our customers, so that all communications, previously solved problems can be retrieved based on keywords, and no new problems or changes need to be forgotten.

If you are looking for e-mail or Skype, we will be very helpful in providing you with a detailed description of the problem and some related screenshots, as well as detailed information on the need for modification and development.

You can also contact your software vendor with questions or requests with confidence (if you purchased the software through a reseller) .

1. Features of the software

 

1.1 Software category

 

Business software.
Astro Business Software is a unique representative of the business software category. Other than traditional management software, or entry-level enterprise management systems *, although software in these categories contains a lot of things, but in fact they are not able to provide the maximum: It is easy to see that a product can only focus on one area at a time. and this is true even if there are multiple versions of it.
Astro Business Software specifically focuses on dealing with the special business processes of merchant and service companies by one hundred percent.
Other important features: compact, fast, clean, easy to handle, low hardware, local and cloud-free, almost without compromise.

Of course, it can be supplemented with custom-made developments like any other software. It contains other industry-specific modules (for example, for manufacturing, servicing, forwarding, optical companies) , but they are already very unique parts of the software and can only be used by a limited range of companies.

* Corporate governance systems are, according to their officially accepted definition, companies of more than 250 employees and more than 50 million euros per year. Very complex software systems that can be customized to companies, can be parameterized to the smallest detail, with their own internal development tools, strong decision support, efficient forecasting functions, and deployed and operated by a complete team of experts. Due to their start-up and maintenance costs, only companies can afford it. In addition to this category, there are entry-level enterprise management systems that are actually management software with a structure similar to ERP systems.

 

1.2. Software architecture

 

This is the software system client-server (client-server) architecture. In this case, this means that you have a database manager server (server) and one or more client software installed on the computer (customer) .

1.2.1. Database Management Server

The database manager is on the server side a server that is responsible for storing and executing data on the data. The server page performs its tasks invisibly to the average user. A business software database managerand MySQL, which is due to its widespread, flexible and low cost.

1.2.2. The client software

The client software (business software) on the client computer (on the user's computer) and connected to the database managerto. In practice, the client software can be identified by the user software with the business software itself. Full-featured native application *, no need to run on any other program, framework, runtime, or web browser. There is no need to install it, just copy it to another computer or flash drive where you can start it immediately.

* Native applications run directly on hardware so they can deliver the best performance, stability, security, solving any demand, connect to other applications, directly access computer resources and use peripherals connected to it.

 

1.3. Software configurations

 

1.3.1. Local installation

The database manager on the same computer as the client softwarealso used. This is the best solution if you use only one copy of your business software and you do not need to be able to access the database anywhere else.

1.3.2. Networked

If you want to use the software on multiple computers on a local network, you may want to install the database manager on a separate server and only the client software on the other computers must be installed.

1.3.3. Remote server installation

A server on one site can be accessed from the local network and from other geographic locations over the Internet for business software running on client computers.

1.3.4. Cloud Computing

We provide the database manager server from our own cloud-based * server. In this case, only client-specific business software should be installed on the client's computers (more precisely, just copy it to a library, because the client is portable and can be started from a flash drive, for example) .

* Cloud computing or original English called cloud computing: 1. The services to be used are provided by a provider's hardware and software tools available over the Internet. 2. Marketing name of profitable IT services available via the Internet.

1.3.5. Based on remote desktop service

The database manager server and client software also run on the same server computer. Only the client software image is transmitted from the server to the user's computer, so the software can be used quickly and safely on client computers with the least bandwidth, with the least bandwidth, uncertain stability.

 

1.4. Basic hardware and software requirements

 

1.4.1. For local installation

·        Intelor AMDbrand processor (some older intel Celeron/ G Series / Atom processors slower than expected due to poor performance under database servers

·        3 or 4Gb + memory ( operating system dependent)

· At        least 500 Mb of free space on the mass storage (more Gb free space is optimal)

·        Windows XP, Vista, 7, 8, 8.1, 10 operating systems

·        1280x800 screen resolutionminimum (1920x1080 is the optimum)

1.4.2. Server (for network and remote server installation)

·        Intelor AMDhigh-performance processor (for example, intel Xeonor intel i7)

·        8, 16Gb + memory (the more the better)

· At        least 1 GB of free space on the mass storage (we need to think about the data, so it is advisable to maintain more than Gb)

·        Fast storage (for example 2x500Gb 10,000 rpm HDDor 2x500Gb SSD)

·        Linuxor Windowsserver operating system

o   Remote Desktop Service only a Windows operating system is recommended for a built-in configuration

o   On Windows Server 2003, driver problems may occur with remote desktop access, so this is only recommended for a database server for remote desktop service no.

·        Installed MySQL Database Management

o   Minimum version 5.5 is recommended, but it is recommended to download the latest version and install it!

o   Database manager for best performance not on a virtual machine Run!

o   The database manager not VPNclients (because it will be slow) , but direct TCP / IP through contact!

o   Some antivirusand other firewalls from non-operating systemscan filter and significantly slow down data traffic (with slow shutdowns, the system should be tested periodically) !

o   The database manager must be open on the server firewall port! This is MySQL TCP port 3306 by default, but it may be different depending on the configuration!

o   If you want to access a server located on the premises of other premises, then the routerset port forwarding to the port mentioned in the previous point and to have a fixed IP address for your central location, or at least use a dynamic DNS service.

o   The database create a user with all rights ('super' or 'all' rights: this is because the software can modify the database and other settings when it automatically updates itself) , and of course it can also be accessed from outside client software running on client computers can connect to it!

1.4.3. For clients

·        Intelor AMDbrand processor

·        2, 3, or 4Gb + memory ( operating system dependent)

· At        least 150 Mb of free disk space

·        Windows XP, Vista, 7, 8, 8.1, 10

·        Running Linux and OS Xon Operating Systems, Wine It helps.

·        1280x800 screen resolution minimum

·        Install Windows Operating Systemt running on a tablet also

 

2. Installation

 

2.1. Installation files

 

The software can be downloaded with automatic installation files, which means that after the launch, without any issues, within a few seconds, the software will be installed and launched. This allows for a first start without a quasi-installation. The automatic installer on the desktop * creates the software client application icon. Later you can start the software with this icon.

* We refer to the desktop of the operating system as the main screen, on which we can freely place application icons and the application windows that are launched.

There are three types of installation files that you can run on Windows (XP, Vista, 7, 8, 8.1, 10) :

2.1.1. For local installation only:

It also installs the database server and the software client on the same computer. This installation can also be used on a server computer.

2.1.2. Server Only:

It only installs the database server.

2.1.3. Workstation Only:

It only installs the client running on the workstations, this version will ask for some settings (server address or cloudy code for cloudy use) . In case of cloudy use, you do not have to install a database server on any of your computers. The demo installer is also a cloudy workstation installer.

You must manually install the MySQL database server on Linux, which can be downloaded from www.mysql.com . The installation of the Linux database server must be done taking into account the rules outlined in section 1.4.

If you already have an installed software client application, you can copy it to any other workstation without installing it.

If you have purchased the software, the installation will be carried out free of charge by the manufacturer at a pre-agreed time. This is not required for cloud-based use, but only for 2.1.3. You must download the client application referred to in point 1.1, start and enter the resulting cloud registration code.

 

2.2. Server connection and cloud-based account settings

 

In cloud-based applications, as already mentioned, only 2.1.3. You will need to install the software client application referred to in point 1.1, and when you start, press the Activation button on the Start screen to enter the exact name of your company (which you provided at the time of purchase) and the cloud activation code. Then, instead of the demo server, the software will be connected to a secure cloud account created for your company.

If you access the database from your own server (private cloud) , you must provide the server connection information. If the software file named uzlet.ini is not found in the software directory, the software will ask for the server's access data.

If the parameter file uzlet.ini exists in the software directory, you can modify the following connection parameters:

MySQL_HostName = "localhost"   - the IP address or domain name of the database server
MySQL_Port = "3306" - the database server TCP port number
MySQL_DataBase = "sulet"    - the name of the database (schema)
MySQL_UserName = "username"    - connection username for the database
MySQL_Password = "password"    - connection password to the database, if it is not specified, the client tries to connect with the default password

 

2.3. Settings for first startup

 

If you have not yet entered all the important corporate data in the settings, the Program Settings window will open , where you can fill in the missing data under the Basic Data tab in the Company Data tab . Each input field has a clear name. If you are a sole proprietor, a taxpayer or a simplified entrepreneur, you need to set up the appropriate switches and data.

The program settings module is available from the main menu in the Settings main menu Data Management group in it (if it does not open automatically) .

At the same time, under the Company Data tab , we recommend that you record the details of your bank account and cashier at the start. If you want to display your company logo on your documents (such as your invoice) , you can find the appropriate image file under the Logo tab . We also recommend setting up corporate email mail server data because the software will use this email address to send balance reports, various notifications, or vouchers (remote billing for an invoice) .

You also need to enter the data required for the NAV Online Account Reporting function in the program settings.

If you first start the software on a new workstation, you need to set the default location, warehouse, and cashier, as these settings may vary by workstation.

 

3. Login

 

After you start the software, you must enter a username and a password to log in. If you are using the software for the first time and you do not already have customized users, you will find the default login information in the email that sent the download link for the software installer.

In the future, more users and employees can be specified in the Master Data Main Menu group , in the HR, in the Employees module , in the same Permissions tab, to restrict or enable certain functions of the system for the user.

You can also select the language in the login window, which can be Hungarian (default), English, German, French, Japanese (only the Hungarian and English versions are available in the basic version).

 

4. Main Screen

 

After you have filled in the correct fields for the sign-in and clicked on the login button, the main screen will appear. If you have already worked on the system and left the modules open, the software will ask you if you want to download the previous session. If you choose to back up the session, you can continue to work where you left off.

 

The main screen consists of the following items:

 

4.1. main Menu

 

On the left, the main menu bar is displayed, and when you move the mouse pointer (or tap it on the touch screen) , the main menu pops up.

The main menus in the main menu are: 

·        Administration - in this menu item you can perform administrative tasks

·        Testimonials, decision support - here we can ask you more important information about the operation of the company

·        Master Data - Record and modify system basic data in the database

·        Settings, data management - system-related settings, export / import functions, data backup, etc. 

·        Exit - log in with another user or exit the system

The main menu groups click when you click on them. Within the main menu groups, the icons of the modules are displayed, logically positioned according to their functions. Clicking on these icons (or tapping on the touchscreen) opens the modules.

Module Icons If you right-click on the left mouse button (or hold your finger on the touch screen for a long time) , you will see a shortcut menu that you can use to add the icon to your favorites.

 

4.2. Main screen window header

 

The title of the main screen window, or the bar at the top of the screen, shows the name of the software, the version number, then the version number of the database in brackets, the software manufacturer, your company name (Registered) , the current location, and the name of the logged in user / employee.

Below, the left side of the header bar shows which module is currently active (or active tab, Module - Almodul) .

 

4.3. Sub-module bar

 

Under the window header, to the left, the sub-module icons of the module to which we have entered are displayed. (Some modules are subdivided into additional sub-modules, such as sub-modules for sales accounts, customer shipping letters, customer receipts, etc.)
On the right, the Favorites icon is located (clicking this will open the [Ctrl + K] combination of the favorites window, keyboard) . the Help icon (which opens a small menu where you can access the manual, version history, license agreement, software contact, and report a problem to customer service) , followed by the current date and time display.

                  

 

4.4. Headband (open, active modules)

 

Below the sub-module bar is the tab bar that shows the active modules that are open.

Some modules open in a separate window (modules that have a special interface) , but most modules have a new tab on the tab bar, as we used in the Internet browser. You can switch between these open modules with tabs. Module tabs can also be moved from one web browser to another, as usual, or locked with the X button on them. This browser schema provides a simple, orderly, and transparent interface.
For most modules (which also appear on the tab bar) , you can see a list of items in that module (in the product body, the products, the invoice list) .

If the tabs do not fit on the screen, you will see two buttons on the right, with a left and one right arrow. This allows you to scroll the ears in both directions.

To navigate between active modules, if you are on the main screen and press the [Esc] key, the module list will appear in the middle, which will also allow you to switch between open modules.

Each module change (if you move from one open module to another) will reload the data of the next module (it may take up to a few seconds) to ensure that you always see the data stored on other workstations.

The Start tab (the first tab from the left) is the only one that is always visible and cannot be closed. After starting the software, you will start with the Start page (unless you have loaded the previous session) on which system information will be displayed and on which you can build a company dashboard from the flash reports (read more on the Modules topic) .

 

4.5. Module toolbar

 

Below the tab bar is the toolbar of the module that we entered. Here are the following buttons:

·        Use the New Record [F2] key to record a new item (for example, a new product in the product body, a new invoice for the invoices) . The edit window for that module will then appear.

·        The Edit [F3] to edit the selected item (this editor window for the module is also displayed) .

·        Use[ Copy F5] to copy an item, so you can create a new one based on an existing one.

·        The Delete [F8] key can be used to delete an item if the system allows it (if other modules are not yet in use or a document is not closed) .

·        Use the Print button to print a list on the screen.

·        Use the Export button to export the list on screen to CSV or Excel Xls / Xlsx.

·        The Representation graphical buttons (various graphs and charts) can display the data in the list (this will be more details below) .

·        Use the Search button to display a simple search window (similar to other software) that you can use to find the column in which the selection is made.

·        Use the Filter button to turn the filter table on and off, which allows you to easily filter the list according to almost any individual needs (see section 4.7 for more information on filter mode) .

·        Use the Custom Lists button to save the aforementioned filtered table view or load a previously saved table.

·        The Details button allows you to activate the item detail view. This is an extra bar within the spreadsheet where you can find all the data for that item. The detail bar is always displayed under the currently selected item. This feature is not available in all modules.

·        The Upgrade button simply reload the list.

·        With Group Operations , if you select multiple items by ticking the check boxes in front of the rows, you can modify certain items at once. Not all modules work.

·        The Balance button will only appear for vouchers, including those whose financial settlement is enabled in the settings. For example, we can specify the amount paid for transfer accounts.

·        The Bank Extract button is only displayed on documents. Opens a sub-module that allows you to scan bank extract files and automatically delete them from the system.

·        The NAV report button (shown only in the Customer Accounts module) will send the not yet reported but reported * invoices to the NAV server with the software (the accounts will be reported automatically after they have been closed, but if for some reason you would not succeed, press this button the report can be forced to start again). This NAV function also runs automatically when you exit the software to make sure you don't miss an unpaid account that day.

* At present, all invoices issued by domestic companies or private contractors, whose VAT content reaches or exceeds HUF 100,000, must be reported.

·        With the Close button you can lock the module in which you have just entered.

        

 

4.6. Featured Filters

 

Below the module toolbar are the highlighted filters. These are always visible and module dependent, which highlighted filters appear here.
For example, in the case of vouchers, the year, month, currency, location.

Important information about featured filters:

·        The filter settings for the highlighted filters are overridden bythe filter mode ( Filter button on the module toolbar ) . For example, in the case of vouchers, the year and month in the highlighted filter are in vain, if we set any time interval in the filter mode enabled, the latter will be considered.

·        On the right side of the highlighted filters bar, there is a quick filter for each module , which is for quick search only, so it does not replace the normal filter mode.

·        In the case of vouchers, you can access the Batch view on the highlighted filter bar, which means that unlike the basic list, another list appears showing the items in the documents and filtering or grouping operations.

 

4.7. Filter Table

 

If the Filter button left on the module toolbar turns on filtering, then a filter table appears between the highlighted filters and the table showing the items in the module.

By using this filtering mode (and in combination with grouping and group summaries), you can create almost any unique statement from our data, which you can also save by clicking the Custom Lists button.

This is a three-line table in which each column has its equivalent in the list. You can freely write or select data in your columns.

Filter rows:

1.     If you write some data to the filter column in the first line of the filter, the exact text or data you enter will be displayed.

2.     If you write something in the second line of the filter, the larger the data will be displayed.

3.     If you type in the third line of the filter, it will filter the smaller data.

Filtering is automatic: as we write or select a data from the drop-down list, the system will constantly change the list.
You can also enter details about the data you want to filter, and then filter some of the matches.
You can use the% character for the data you type in the filters, for example, to filter for word match and end word.

You can write filter criteria (even to each one) in multiple columns at once .

In case of date filtering, a small calendar can be dropped, but the data to be filtered can also be entered manually. You can confirm the date filter by pressing the [Enter] key on the keyboard and pressing [Backspace] or [Del].

If you disable the filter table with the Filter button on the toolbar, the filtering will also be canceled and the list will return to normal mode.

You can delete the filter conditions at the same time without turning off the filter by clicking on the small icon in the top left corner of the filter table or by using the [Ctrl + R] key combination.

 

4.8. Item lists

 

Most of the main screen is the list of items (or data) of the current module (in which we are currently working) .

This is a standard table with arbitrary grouping and sub-aggregation options.

Below we discuss the use and configuration options of the item lists:

 

         4.8.1. Column Settings

 

The column headers can be moved freely with the mouse by grabbing another position and adjusting their width.

You can set up one or more columns to increase or decrease in order. You can change the order and its direction by clicking on the column header (left mouse button) , or the same can be set in the right-click menu of the column header , also in the direction of sorting .

Because we can sort the list data by multiple columns at once, all sorted columns have priority. First, sorted by Priority 1, within the Priority 2 column and so on. Sort priorities in the column header menu above, in the column priority section. Column priority also plays a role in grouping (below) .

Arranging multiple columns at the same time may slow down the query of the lists (this is most often the case for calculated columns that cannot be indexed as the native columns) . If you detect a slowdown, you can only sort the data in a row by one column, turn off the order with the rest by using the no sort option in the column header menu.

If you click the right mouse button (long touch on the touchscreen) with a column header instead of any other part of the table where there are data, another pop-up menu will appear that allows you to turn the columns' visibility on and off, and save your column settings as default .

Below the list is the end summary bar, where the totals are displayed by columns.

If you right-click on this summary bar, you will see a pop-up menu where you can set the type of aggregation for that column (this also applies to grouping sub-totals) .

                                                      

 

         4.8.2. Grouping and sub-aggregations

 

A faster, more transparent, server-side, multi-level grouping and sub-aggregation algorithm has been introduced in the software.

For example, our documents can be grouped by customer and serial number. These can be further grouped in item view, for example, by item group, product group, or VAT key.

Columns can be grouped by right-clicking in the header of the column you want to group , and then selecting Grouping by Column in the pop-up menu that appears . Then the data will be grouped by this column (also).
Similarly, you can disable the grouping on that column.
The grouping columns are always automatically placed at the top of the table.

You can enable or disable subgroup summaries in the same context menu by selecting Grouping aggregates . Enabling sub-totals for each grouping column applies simultaneously.
You can select the type of column totals (none, aggregate, average, etc.) as you would otherwise, by clicking the right mouse button on the shortcut menu that appears below the table.

The grouping order of the grouped columns can also be set in the above-mentioned menu, in the " Column Priority " section, or by grabbing the column at the header and dragging it to the correct position.

You can also specify the direction of sorting by grouping from the context menu in the " Sort Direction " section or by clicking on the column header with the left mouse button.

Each group level is represented by a different color, so you can easily distinguish between the different levels in the list.

Saving the lists, including saving the default list view, also saves the grouping settings.

 

         4.8.3. Select status, multiple rows

 

A narrow column is shown on the left side of each table. This is the so-called indicator column.

In the indicator column, there is a square at the beginning of each line, and if you click it, a check mark appears , marking the line. You can remove the selection by clicking again. Pressing [Space] also switches the line selection. By clicking on the icon in the upper left corner of the table , you can select the entire list, or you can also undo the selections from each line. If you select multiple rows, you can perform group actions at the same time. Group actions can be performed on the toolbar by pressing the Group Actions button, if available in that module.
 

Each row in the indicator column may have different colors, depending on the status of that row. The statuses and colors vary by module.
For example, in the case of vouchers, yellowed drafts are highlighted , unbalanced red , green are matched and completed , the turquoise is invalidated and corrected .

 

4.9. Graphic representation of item list data

 

Use the Display button on the module toolbar to graphically display graphs (graphs and charts) in the list.

It is advisable to first filter the list according to our needs before depicting the data.

From the drop-down list named chart diagram , you can select the settings you have previously saved.
You can save the current settings with the small disk icon.
Press the red X button to delete the saved settings.

To the right there are six more buttons in the function line:

·        Turn on and off isometric 3D view

·        Redraw and reset the figure

·        Print

·        Save as Image (in BMP format)

·        Frameless full screen mode

·        Exit display

The following line item can be used to render display settings:

·        Chart type , which can be graph, bar graph, bar graph, area charts, scatter plots, pie chart

·        X and Y data : in these settings you can specify which data to display on the two axes

·        Grouping : specify how the data should be aggregated (recommended for many data)

 

 

5. Modules

 

There are almost 100 modules and sub-modules in the software (the number of which is constantly increasing, so a module may be added later in the manual) . These include administrative functions, various vouchers, decision support, master data, and auxiliary functions, system maintenance.

This section reviews the modules in a row, by category. (Some custom modules may be missing from this version of this manual, such as the modules for the forwarder and optics system)

Within the categories, the modules will be listed in order of their construction.

There are modules that are very similar and differ only in a few of their parameters, only the differences will be mentioned in the manual.

Let's start with a special module:

 

5.1. Start tab

 

The Start tab module is always active and cannot be closed. You can access it on the tab bar by clicking on the first Start tab.

                   On the Start tab, you'll find the following features:

 

5.1.1. system Messages

 

The System tab is displayed in the upper left corner of the Start tab. The System Messages panel displays inventory shortages, alerts that are not closed, and other important information.
If you click on a message in the System Messages panel, the module with the alert is automatically opened after a question.
Here is highlighted in purple if there are no accounts yet.

        

 

5.1.2. Quick reports, company dashboard

 

On the Start tab, use the button on the sub-module bar to create quick report windows. These flash report windows can be moved and resized in the same way as any other standard window. By defining multiple flash report windows, we can build an informative dashboard that continuously monitors the company's operations. 

In the quick report window header , you can specify in the setup menu that appears by clicking on the icon (in the lower left corner the bottom left corner) the format in which you want to display the data, the type of report, the period, the type of values, and the location. The Quick Report Setup menu also appears when you right-click on that window. 

Use the button to confirm the quick report settings. 

Speed ​​report data is automatically updated every 15 minutes, including system messages. With this ideal time interval, the server is less burdened, but still enough to track the status of the company in almost real time.

You can also manually start the update of the flash report data by clicking the button. You can sort the report windows in a row by pressing.  

If the flash reports do not fit on the "Start" tab, the internal size of the page increases and scroll bars can access windows that are hanging or not visible from the page.

Quick reports are saved when you exit the program and recharge if you choose to load the previous session at login. At this point, signing up will take a little extra time to update your flash report data.

Quick Reporting can only be used by users who have access to "Statements, Decision Support" between permissions.

Firmware dashboard built from quick report windows on the "Start" tab:

 

5.2. Master data

 

In order for the system to work, there must be some basic data such as the VAT strain, partner strain, employees, product groups, product strain, etc. Some types of master data already contain data that needs to be modified or expanded if necessary, but most master data is still empty, and we need to upload them.

 

5.2.1. VAT tribe

 

This module is used to manage the existing VAT types. The current VAT types are already included in the installation.
The VAT rates shown in the VAT invoice can be selected in the product groups and in the product body (for each product).

If a new VAT type is introduced, it must be recorded as a new entry.
If a VAT type ceases to exist, it will not be deleted because it may still be referred to by previous vouchers (such as invoices, shipping notes).
Whenever you change the VAT rate, follow the advice above, do not override the value of the previous VAT key, but instead create a new entry. The exchange of VAT rates for products (goods or services) can be solved in other ways.

You can edit the following data:

·        Name: enter the official name of the VAT in this field. This VAT text will also appear on your invoice. Required data.

·        Key: enter the numerical value of VAT in this field, as this will account for the system. If you are editing a commonly used VAT type (currently 27% VAT), you can tick the "Default" option. Required data.

·        Type of tax: there are special types of VAT, these have to be marked separately for the software under "Tax type". For these special types of VAT, it is mandatory to include on the documents (eg invoice) clause text per item (Item Comment) or at the end of the document (Document Note).

Here are the options:

o   Reverse taxation

o   Tax-free

o Excluded from   the VAT Act

o   Taxed according to the difference including the passed tax

o   Taxes that do not include a pass-through tax

o   Default: we can set the VAT to be the default, ie when we add a new product group or a new product in the product body, it automatically offers this VAT. This setting will be overwritten by the VAT set for the selected product group for the product. By default, the most commonly used VAT is set. If a VAT is set as the default, this setting will be deleted from the previous default VAT.

·        Item Note: If you are required to include the clause text in the invoices for each item in the VAT, you can enter its exact text in this field.

·        Document note: if you are required to include a clause text in the note note for the relevant VAT, you can enter its exact text in this field.

If you have entered all the data accurately, you can use the "Save" button to record the new VAT to the VAT base.

                           

                                     

5.2.2. Gender Gender (or Titles)

                                     

We will always have to choose a title for our bank and cashier cash flows. This master data module is used to store these types of item.

As suggested for the VAT strain, deletion is not recommended here either.

We must provide the following information:

·        Name: the exact, official text of the money transaction.

·        Direction: We need to determine whether this particular cash flow is a revenue or an expense.

There are special types of cash flow, such as customer or supplier payment . In this case, we need to give the appropriate option that the software knows, for example, that for a given cash flow we have settled the account of a buyer or supplier.

If all data is entered correctly, you can use the "Save" button to record the new title in the master data.

                                  

                                     

5.2.3. cost centers

                                    
In order to account for our costs, we need cost centers that can be created in this module.

A cost center consists of two data:

·        Code: code of the cost center. This code can be used to refer to that cost center.

·        Name : full name of the cost center. In addition to the code, this name can also be used to refer to the cost center.

The cost centers defined here can be selected when editing the items in the documents (for example, items on an incoming invoice).

 

5.2.4. locations

 

A company may have more than one location, so any number of sites can be created in the software.

When you add a new location, you can fill in the following information:

·        Site ID: freely definable number data. When recording a new site, the module always offers the next number. Required data.

·        Site Type: Central or Branch / Client. The site type can play a role in special cases, for example, if you run a webshop and send the products to the customers from the central site, the software can decide on the basis of this setting which of these is the central site.

·        Name: Name of the site. This name can be used to refer to other sites in the site. Required data.

·        Address data: the title is divided into basic data. Address information is optional but recommended.

·        Tel / Fax: the telephone contact details of the site can be listed here.

If necessary, this module may be expanded upon request with other information such as site manager or any other data.

                                     

5.2.5. warehouses

 

Our products are stored in warehouses within the premises. You can have more than one warehouse on one site. In this module, you can create these stores.

When creating a new warehouse, you need to provide the following information:

·        Site: the location where the warehouse is located. Required data.

·        Warehouse name: the name of the warehouse, it will be selectable at any location where you want to refer to the warehouse. Required data.

·        Price category: The price of the selected price category will be offered by the software at the time of sale (if selected in the price range of the selected partner in the voucher).

·        Warehouse type: important, mandatory data type is the type of warehouse that is basic information for the software. These types may include:

o   Normal: general warehouse (in case of new warehouse it is recommended by software).

o   Transit: a transit logistic warehouse where the products are only temporarily on paper (for example, as an intermediate logical storage warehouse for transfer from one truck to another).

o   Commercial: In this type of warehouse, we can store commissioned products.

o   Disposal: In this type of warehouse, we store the scraps (only scraps of this type can be selected as a target warehouse for scrapping).

o   Waste: a storage facility for waste generated during production.

o   Background: is a special store where we store products that have been set aside, lying or for other purposes.

o   Service: for storage of repaired, repairable, repaired and repaired goods that are waiting for delivery.

·        Archive Warehouse: Check this if the warehouse is no longer in use (instead of deleting the warehouse).

·        Warehouse Storage: All products in a warehouse have their own place. These so-called repositories, which can be defined in the product data sheet on the product data sheet for each product. Here, the storage modules are only listed in the warehouse module.

 

5.2.6. Product fees (environmental product charges)

 

In the cases specified in the law and government decree on environmental product charge , the invoices shall contain itemized product fees and the text of the clause required by the decree.

These product fees can be managed in this module. The product fees created here can be assigned to each product in the product body (for products, it will also be necessary to indicate the quantity of a basic unit of the product in kilograms of the given product charge).

When creating individual product charges, all data is required :

·        Type: Product Type is an important piece of information for the software that can be:

o   Packaging agent

o   Waste

o   Product fee

·        Code: The code for the product fee is a code line consisting of letters and numbers with hyphens that must be specified.

·        Fee: the fee for packaging, waste or other product charge in kilograms, expressed in HUF, must also be specified.

·        Name: indicate the exact name of the packaging, waste or other product charge.

·        Closing text: this field must be precisely preceded by the clause defined by the Regulation.

More information about each field will appear if you hold the mouse cursor over them. A local help menu is displayed on the module if you hold the mouse pointer over the blue information icon in the upper right corner.

It is the responsibility of the user to fill in the product data correctly.

 

5.2.7. product Groups

 

In the product body, we will have to provide a product group for each product. In this module, we can create and edit these product groups.

The following information can be provided:

·        Group code: this is an arbitrary number. The module will automatically offer you the following. Required data.

·        Name of the group : name of the product group. This name will appear everywhere, so we can refer to the product group. Required data.

·        Parent group: The most important feature of product groups is that they are located in a structure tree. Therefore, each group has a parent group that must be selected. If a group does not have a parent group, that is, a parent root group, then this should be chosen instead of the parent: "Root group". Required data.

·        Root Group Marker Color: Optional color for root groups can also be specified, which will be relevant to the statements in the Revenue Chart.

·        Character: additionalimportant data to be selected is the nature of the group, which determines which products are found in the group.
Characteristics include:

o   Commodity: general commodity.

o   Service: if you have any service.

o   Prepayment: If you want to give an advance payment, then there must be at least one prepayment entry in the product body, as the invoice must include an advance as a separate item.

o   Packaging: any other product used to store products.

o   Packaging: packaging material (this is not the same as the product fee data, here as a product).

o   Waste: retained from production or other waste (not equal to the data that can be specified in the product fee module, it is listed here as a product).

o   Land vehicle

o   Air transport

o   Water transport device

o   Tobacco product

o   Mineral oil

o   Shipping costs: If you want to charge for shipping costs, you will need to include a shipping cost item in the product body, as this item must be shown on the invoices as a separate item.

·        VAT: this data is the same as the character, the products in the edited group will be subject to the specified VAT (but can be set separately for the products). The VAT data can be selected from the items recorded in the VAT base. Required data.

·        Price category settings: Price category settings are also inherited for products in the group (for products, you can also change the base price category code and profit margin individually).
The default settings for the price categories can be changed in the software settings. Here you can only change the base price category code of the price categories and the profit margin (the name and currency of the categories can only be edited in the settings).
It is important to mention that price categories can be programmed for automatic calculations. To do this, you must enter the code for the category in the "basic price category code" column of the price category you are automatically calculating (the first column shows the category codes) from which you would like the price of the current category to be calculated and the percentage that the base is in the profit margin field. must be added to the price of your category.
In which price category the "basic price category code" column has its own code, that category will not be automatically calculated.

If you have changed the settings for a group price category and want the products under the group to be inherited immediately, you need to press the button below the table.

·        Show in the webshop: If the software is connected to a webshop, you can set whether the category is displayed in the webshop, orbrowsethe category image tab to display the category image, also in the group webshop.

In addition to the basic data, the property profile tab allows you to create a list of unique properties for the products in the group (whose values ​​can be filled in for each product).
Here you can use the value in the order column to create the order of displaying properties, and in the title column you can type the property name.

 

5.2.8. product Families

 

Each product has a manufacturer, and manufacturers usually classify their products into families. This module is used to store these product families at the master data level.

We only need to enter two data:

·        Manufacturer: a manufacturer type manufacturer. If there is no manufacturer to choose from, you can use the first button to create a new producer in the partner body to the right of this field.

·        Name: the name of the product family.

For products, we can also specify the manufacturer and the product line freely, without the need to record this data as master data. In this, the software gives free hand.

 

5.2.9. Product Master Data

 

In this module, we can list and edit products and services (services can be considered a kind of product).

The products are discussed in two parts in this chapter. One looks at the list of products and the other is the product datasheet on which we can edit the details of each product.

 

5.2.9.1. List of products

 

When we enter the product body module, we have the following settings above the list of products (marked in red on the picture):

·        Discontinued: If you enable this option, products that have been set aside previously will also appear in the list.

·        Inventory: lets you filter the list by following these criteria:

o   All products: no stock filtering.

o   Positive quantities only: only products that have a stock appear in the selected warehouse.

o   Negative quantities only: only shows the products for which the inventory level has turned negative in the selected warehouse (this is possible if the sale is ahead of receipt).

o   Decreased only below the minimum set: only products in the inventory that are in stock have fallen below the minimum specified for the selected warehouse.

·        Product Group: If you only want to list products that belong to a particular product group (and its descended groups), you must select the appropriate group here.

·        Warehouse: Here you can select the warehouse for which the previous filters work and the values ​​of the special stock level indicator columns are calculated.

For more information about the special stock level indicator columns (marked in blue):

Ø The minimum set column in the warehouse above the list shows the minimum set limit set for the product.

Ø The stock column shows the current stock level in the selected warehouse.

Ø All bookings that are not yet completed (not yet invoiced) can be seen in the Reservations column.

Ø In the ordered column, we can see all the quantities recorded on the unsubscribed (non-received) supplier's order.

If you want to see more information about a product in the list, you can see the details bar by turning on the details mode . There are several tabs on this bar: 

·        Product Details: You can see the main image of the product and read other basic data.

·        Product Description: A preview of the html format edited on the product data sheet is displayed.

·        Product Kit: Here you can see in a table by stock what quantities the product has, which warehouse contains the minimum, optimum, maximum inventory level settings and which storage locations the product can find.

·        Product purchases: You can see when and how much was taken from the product by date.

·        Product Sales: By date you can see when and how much of the product was sold.

 

5.2.9.2. Product datasheet

 

If you create a new product or edit an existing one, the product data sheet will appear:

This is one of the most complex modules. Product data can be divided into several groups, available on separate tabs (tabs):

/   Basic Data Sheet

On this page, we need to start filling in the data even more, because there are required fields:

·        Product code: Product code. In the case of a new product, the next serial number is automatically assigned, but can be rewritten. Its value can consist of letters and numbers. Required data.

·        Retail Webshop: If you run a webshop, you can set up the product to be added to the retail store.

·        Wholesale Webshop: If you run a webshop, you can set up the product to be uploaded to the shop's wholesale section.

·        Preorderable: this option is used to indicate if the product is not yet available but pre-ordered. It is also important in a webshop.

·        Highlighted: will show the product among the highlighted products of the webshop if this switch is activated.

·        Discontinued: If the product is no longer available, it will not be marketed with this switch (instead of product deletion). Later, when the product is available again, we can turn it off again.

·        Parent Product: If there are more, almost identical products that differ only in a few parameters, you can select one as the product of the other parent. This is useful, for example, in a webshop for a more transparent presentation of products.

·        Product group: Product groups have been mentioned before. Here you can select the appropriate product group for your product. After selecting the product group, the group's nature, VAT setting and price category settings will be copied to this product (these data can then be changed). Required data.

·        + Groups: If you run a webshop, you can use this feature to classify your product into several product groups.

·        Character: additional mandatory information on the nature of the product. This product is inherited from the product group (but can be changed afterwards). Characteristics include:

o   Commodity: general commodity.

o   Service: if you have any service.

o   Prepayment: If you want to give an advance payment, then there must be at least one prepayment entry in the product body, as the invoice must include an advance as a separate item.

o   Packaging: any other product used to store products.

o   Packaging: packaging material (this is not the same as the product fee data, here as a product).

o   Waste: retained from production or other waste (not equal to the data that can be specified in the product fee module, it is listed here as a product).

o   Land vehicle

o   Air transport

o   Water transport device

o   Tobacco product

o   Mineral oil

o   Shipping costs: If you want to charge for shipping costs, you will need to include a shipping cost item in the product body, as this item must be shown on the invoices as a separate item.

·        VTSZ: the heading to be given for the article, not required, except where it is necessary for a particular product.

·        SZJ / TESOR: service list number is also optional, unless required for some reason.

·        VAT: the VAT rate of the product. It is inherited from the product group after its selection (but can be changed later). Required data.

·        Manufacturer: The manufacturer is a “manufacturer” type partner that must be affixed to the partner body. But it is not necessary to select from the partner body;

·        Product family: the product family can be selected from the strain, but can also be given in free words.

·        Product name: one of the most important features of the product is its name. Be sure to fill out. Required data.

·        Item number: Product article number (or SKU). The button next to it can generate a new one or be freely filled. If left blank, a new article number is generated for the product when you save the product. Item number generation is based on a mask that can be defined in the settings.

·        Barcode: The product barcode has similar rules to the item number. You can type (or retrieve) the product's serial number or create a new one with the button next to it (the mask can be defined in the settings). It is definitely helpful if we handle product article numbers, because using barcode scanners can significantly speed up your receipt, inventory, inventory management, and sales.

·        Default unit: The default unit of measure to calculate a unit quantity of product. The most common units can be selected from the list, but can also be filled in freely. If the product does not have a unit of measurement (for example, in the case of an advance or shipping cost), then we can do a dash character here. Required data.

 

The Basic Data tab includes additional tabs:

 

¤ Properties tab

On this page, you can record the most important features of the product (these are optional):

§   Color: the characteristic color of the product.

§   Size Range: If a product has a range of characteristics, it can be specified here. There is no specific unit of measure because it can be used for many purposes.

§   Weight: The net and gross weight of the product in kilograms. You can also specify a broken value.

§   Length, width, height, diameter: the spatial dimensions of the product, in millimeters.

§   Category (other): If necessary, we can freely specify other category data for the product.

§   Warranty: If we manage the warranty period for the product, you can specify the length of the warranty period in years and months.

§   Guarantor: if we manage a warranty for the product but the warranty holder is another company, we can choose the person from the partner body.

§   Age: If the product is characterized by what age it is recommended for, then the age range can be specified here.

§   Webshop product name: If you are running a webshop, you may want to display a different name from the official name of the product on the interface of the shop (for example, an abbreviated name to extract). When we change the name of the product, this data is automatically filled in if it is empty. Use the button next to it to copy the full name of the product.

 

 

 

¤ Prices tab

Here you can specify the price categories and make other pricing options:

§   Outlet Price: This is a special setting, deducting 20 percent of the price.

§   Gross rounding: defines the rounding method when calculating gross prices from the net prices of the table.

§   Automatic price calculation: If off, prices will not be calculated automatically.

§   Price table: Here you can specify the product prices by price category. In case of a new product, the basic settings of the price category are inherited from the settings (category name, currency) and from the selected product group (basic price category code, profit margin). The base price category code, profit margin, net and gross price can be changed.

If the automatic price calculation is turned on, if we change a price, all other prices will be recalculated (multiplied by the profit margin) where the code for that price category is given as the base price category code.

You can also change the product prices on the vouchers, but in the case of a new voucher item, you will always be offered a price that matches the selected price range of the selected partner or warehouse.

A similar price calculation table can also be found in the document batch editor.

 

¤ Storage Settings tab

On this page, we can configure the minimum inventory, optimum, and maximum inventory levels for the different warehouses. The "Current" column also shows the current product set.

You can add storage space to your product under the Warehouse table. The product will be found in these repositories. The repositories will also be listed in the Inventory Invoice and Warehouse Release Documents entry note.

Add Warehouse Storage:

1.     First, you need to select the warehouse to which you want to add storage space in the top table .

2.     Then, you can create a new line with the New Storage button in the upper left corner of the storage table .

3.     In the new line , you can enter any code in the code column that you can use to link to the storage space.

In the storage name column, you can write your storage location, for example: 1st row, 2nd column, 5th shelf

In the maximum quantity column, you can write the quantity that fits the product in that storage space.

The maximum load column is the storage capacity of the storage space.

 

 

 

¤ Transport Device Settings tab

If the product is a means of transport, it may be necessary to provide the corresponding data. In the case of a means of transport, these data are the data to be included in the invoices.

§   Engine capacity: land, water, aircraft.

§   Engine power: can also be used for land, water, aircraft.

§   Take-off weight: data to be provided for aircraft.

§   Air Commercial Instrument: If the aircraft is engaged in commercial transport activities, it should be marked.

§   Length of vessel: data to be provided for water transport means.

§   Water activity: in the case of water transport means its activity, in writing.

 

¤ Accounting and Tax Information Sheet

Use this tab to enter the product's accounting information.

§   Supplier Ledger Account Number: The ledger number for which the product can be posted from an Inbound (Supplier) Account. Useful information for bookkeeping software (bookkeeping for data submission, or for the built-in built-in automatic accountancy module).

§   Customer ledger account number: The ledger number for which the product can be posted from the outgoing (customer) account. Also useful for accounting program.

§   Cost center: You can choose from the cost centers recorded in the previously discussed cost center module. Information required for cost accounting, accounting.

§   Excise tax content: if the product is subject to excise duty, the amount of tax must be given here, in HUF.

§   Public health product tax: if the product is subject to a public health product tax, its amount must be entered here in HUF.

§   Energy tax: if the product is subject to an energy tax, its amount must be given here in HUF.

§   Tobacco product retail price: In the case of a tobacco product, the retail price set here must be given in HUF.

§   Mediated service: if the product is an intermediary service, then it must be marked. The data is also shown on the invoices.

 

¤ Environmental Product Charges tab

If the product includes an environmental product charge, we can add the product fees fixed in the product fee module, assign it to the product, or more.

How to assign a new product charge:

1.     The New Item button displays the product-product fee editor panel.

2.     Select the product fee you want to assign to the product based on the code or name . If there is no product fee yet, you can use the button to the right of the title field to create a new product fee in the product fee strain. Then click the button to change the product fee in the product fee strain. Caution! If we change a product fee in the product fee stack, it will also affect other products to which we have assigned the same product fee!

3.     Enter how much of the product charge is included in the product unit , in kilograms.

4.     Use the Save button to save the product fee to the list.

The new item non-sphericity right, the Edit edit button in the Delete key to delete the product in the product charge assignment.

The product fee will also appear on the invoice for products that have been assigned a product charge.

 

¤ Storage Data Sheet

You can enter information about the storage method on this tab.

§   Piece / package: you can specify how many pieces of the product are in the package.

§   Package / Cardboard: You can specify how much of a product is contained in a carton. The package / package and package / carton settings may be relevant for individual requirements (custom-made features), where the packaged products and materials in the package must be separated for separate sales.

§   Packaging: type and specification of packaging. Free text data. The previously entered data can be selected the next time.

§   Shipping and storage regulations: Shipping and storage regulations can be listed here. Free text data. The previously entered data can be selected the next time.

 

/   Set sheet

Elements of the product kit (also known as batch, charge, or batch) are listed on this page.

Use the New Batch button on the toolbar of the tab to create a new set of items for the product (the stock items, also called batches, are discussed in the kit module). Use the Edit button to change the item of the selected product, and to delete it using the Delete button.

The warehouse filter can be used to select which warehouse items we want to see for the product.

The total stock of the selected warehouse is shown below the table.

The set is primarily managed by vouchers, here we make corrections and corrections.

 

/   Production Data, Recipe Sheet

If the product is manufactured (or assembled or disassembled) by our own company, the manufacturing data and the recipe (raw materials, raw materials for the production of the product or the parts necessary for its assembly) can be recorded on this sheet.

Production data for manufacturing technology data:

§  Drawing number: the number of the technical drawing to be used for the manufacture of the product.

§  Use and handling instructions: Information about the use of the product or special treatment (eg heat treatment). Free text data that can be selected next for other products.

§  Production weight: the weight of the product at the time of manufacture, in kilograms.

§  Size of the material: the typical dimensions of the raw material needed for the production of the product.

§  Quality: the quality requirements of the product or its raw material.

§  UN number : The UN number (usually 4 digits) denotes the dangerous substances.

§  Net active substance: content of the net active substance of chemicals, pharmaceuticals or pyrotechnic articles, together with the indication of quantity unit.

In the manufacturing recipe, the ingredients or ingredients of the product to be manufactured and the quantities needed to manufacture a unit product can be partially fixed.
The raw materials are also products that must also be in the product body.

To add a new component, follow these steps:

1.     Afterpressingthe New Item button, the Component Editor panel appears.

2.     Select the component by its name. The ingredients are also listed in the product body. If you do not find the component you are looking for (if it does not exist in the product body), you can use the small button on the right side of the data field to create a new product.

3.     Specify the quantity of component required toproduce a unit product(this is the quantity in the default unit quantity of the ingredient).

4.     Save the component that appears in the list.

Use the Edit button next to the New Item button to edit, and the Delete button to delete the component from the list.

Manufacturing (assembly and dismantling) can be done in the manufacturing module.

 

/   Pictures tab

You can attach any number and size of the product here. Images can be in jpg, jpeg, bmp, png, gif. There is no size limit, but we should attach as small images as possible, because larger images slow down the operation of the software (when displaying images). Only images smaller than 2 MB will be saved in the database, larger ones will only be attached to their original location.

 You can attach a new image using the "Browse" button. After selecting the image to be attached, it will appear immediately in the list on the left and in the large preview image on the right.

To delete an image from the list , select the image you want to delete from the list on the left and press the "Delete" button.

The pictures should be between at least one characteristic of the main image , you can select it to select the candidate list to the left image and press the "Next Image" button. Then the main image will be the first in the list. The main image will appear in the list of products when you turn on the details mode.

You can write a subtitle under the images (in the text box below the large view on the right). This caption is automatically saved next to the image.

It is possible to copy the images of another product to the currently edited product by clicking "Copy images of another product" in the upper right corner.

If our company operates a webshop, then it is possible to upload these images to the webshop.

 

/   Description tab

If you want to record text information about the product, you can use this feature on this page.

The description is in HTML format (like web pages) and is saved in the database.

As with standard word processors, you can edit the text so we don't discuss it separately.

You can also insert links and images into the text (shown on the small toolbar, right-click with buttons 4 and 2), but then make sure that these links and images are on the web.

The description is automatically saved when you save the product.

The description can be used, for example, for webshops, for displaying text below or next to products, so that we can pass on more information to customers.

If this description feature does not work or is unavailable, you need to verify that you have Internet Explorer or Edge installed on your system, as this feature uses the snap-in engine of these browsers to display and edit the HTML document.

 

/   Suppliers tab

On this page, we have the option to provide a default supplier and record individual product numbers and barcodes used by our various partners to identify the product.

The default vendor can be selected at the top of the page, from the list of partners. The default vendor setting is useful if you select this partner on a vendor order and press the "Order by Order" button on the toolbar above the item list. In this case, an item list will automatically be created for the order from the products from which the stock level has fallen below the minimum level, so we can start the process of replacing the stock shortage in a few steps. If we turn on the "Send by Email" feature on this supplier order, the partner will receive our order by email when the order is closed (if you have an email address).

To record the unique article numbers and barcodes of our partners, you first need to select from the list on the left the contact whose data you want to record and to enter the appropriate article numbers and barcodes on the right, even listing more.

 

/   Accessories tab

On this page, you can add accessories to the product. Accessories are also products that are related to this product.

To add a new accessory to your product, follow these steps:

1.     Afterpressingthe New Item button, the supplied editor panel appears.

2.     Select the accessory from the Accessories drop-down list. If the product you are looking for is not found in the product body, you can create a new product with the right button first.

3.     Next, you must specify the quantity of the accessory product in its default quantity unit.

4.     Select the accessory type:

o   Optional accessory (if the accessory is sold separately).

o   The main product already contains this accessory at the factory .

o   Must be sold together with the main product, but shown as a separate item .

5.     Use the Save button to save the accessory that will appear in the accessory list.

Use the Edit button next to the New Item button to edit, and the Delete button to delete the selected accessory from the list.

If we run a webshop, the accessory data is also uploaded to the shop database, so customers will see what other products belong to this product.

 

/   Substitute Products Tab

The replacement product is used to replace the replacement product if it is not in stock or left out of the original product.

To create a new replacement product, follow these steps:

1.     Afterpressingthe New Item button, the Substitute Item Editor panel appears.

2. In     theData field labeled Product, selectthe replacement product.

3.     The Save button to save, then the replacement product will appear in the list.

Use the Edit button next to the New Item button to edit, and the Delete button to delete the selected item in the list.

If you run a webshop, the replacement product data will be uploaded to the shop database so that customers will see which similar products can replace the product.

 

/   Extra Data Sheet

For product groups, you can create a unique property list. We can add value to these qualities here.

On the left you can see the properties and edit the values ​​on the right.

To edit the data, simply click on the appropriate line and enter anything freely. Edit with the ENTER key.
The data you provide here will be selected next to the drop-down list.

If you run a webshop, this property list can be uploaded to the shop database and displayed next to the product.

 

If you have provided all the necessary data for the product, you can save it with Save . The product will then appear in the product body list.
In order to save the new product or to cross the other pages, the following mandatory data must be filled out (not empty) on the Basic Data tab :

ü Product code

ü Product group

ü Character

ü VAT

ü Product name

ü Barcode

 

You can also see a label print button at the bottom of the window , after which the label printer window appears with the product name and the selected price.
Label printing can be done with a special label printer.

There is another button called Catalog , which after clicking on the price list appears.
The price catalog is a special custom module that can be used to create custom product configurations with unique prices.
This price catalog should be used in specific areas where the properties or equipment of the products determine its parameters and pricing (for example, optical glasses, car sales, etc.).

If you have modified a product but do not want your data to be stored, you can quit the Cancel button. At this point, only the basic data will not be saved, but the related data will be (because they will be included in the database at the time of the modification).

 

5.2.10. stock holdings

 

This module is used to list and directly modify inventory items (batches).

Basically, you do not need to edit your inventory data, because the software automatically manages your warehouse revenue, warehouse release, warehouse inventory movement, and inventory records. Inventory-related lists (inventory list, inventory, average margins, stock card) are listed under Statements, Decision Support , and Inventory Lists .

This module is discussed in two parts in this chapter. First, you will find a list of inventory items and then a datasheet for editing your stock items (batches).

 

5.2.10.1. List of inventory items

 

When we enter the stock module, a list of stock items (batches) stored in the inventory is displayed.
The following highlighted filters appear above the list:

Ø Only products in stock: display only stock items with stock levels greater than zero.

Ø Warehouse: The warehouse in which the inventory items (batches) are displayed in the list.

If you want to see more information about a product in the list, you can see the details bar by turning on the details mode . The following tabs appear on this bar: 

Ø Production vouchers: Production vouchers appear in the list that created the stock item (batch).

Ø Warehouse Release Documents: Warehouse voucher vouchers appear in the list where a specific quantity was issued from the stock item.

Ø Shipment (Outbound): Outbound shipment vouchers for which we have issued a quantity from the stock item.

Ø Invoices (outbound): Outgoing invoices for which we have issued a quantity of inventory item are displayed.

 

5.2.10.2. Editing inventory items

 

If you want to modify a stock item or register a new item, the edit window will appear.

Be careful when editing inventory items. If not, do not edit the set directly. Editing or deleting inventory items can be done at your own risk.

Here is the following information:

·        Commodity: this is where you can choose the product to which the stock item belongs.

·        Batch (Charge or Lot) identification code: identifier of the stock item (batch), can be used as a charge or LOT number. If this field is blank and we cannot copy anything here, you can use the Generate Code button to create a new LOT number for the rows.

·        Custom properties: here you can write a unique attribute or comment to the stock item.

·        Batch Creation Date: The day the stock item was created.

·        Production time / start of warranty: If the product is manufactured by your own company, the date of manufacture is here, otherwise the warranty period.

·        Warranty / Warranty Expires: The date on which the Product loses its warranty or warranty.

·        Input unit price (net): If the product was purchased from a supplier, it will be included here at its net purchase price.

·        Entry unit price (gross): the net unit price multiplied by the VAT value set on the product.

·        Used: if used items are included in the stock item.

·        Discontinued: if the item contains decomposed products.

·        Damaged: if the stock item contains damaged but still functioning, marketable products.

·        Work count: work sets can be used to link different inventory items with production and vouchers.

·        Warehouse: the warehouse where the item is located.

·        Storage: is the storage space on which the stock item is located within the warehouse.

·        Opening Kit: The stock of stock item stock.

·        Inventory Quantity: The quantity of stock in the stock item in stock.

·        Factory Numbers: A list of individual factory numbers for products in the stock item. All serial numbers are added to a new line.

When you have finished editing the stock item, you can save the data with the Save button. If you just viewed the item details, go to Cancel or simply close the window with the x in the upper right corner.

 

5.2.11. Specials

 

In the action module, you can define discount actions for a particular product, product group, or warehouse.

First we list the action list, then the action editor window.

 

5.2.11.1. List of actions

 

Entering the action module displays the list of actions.

The following high-priority filters are listed above the list:

Ø Closed: actions that have already been completed are displayed.

Ø Actual: actions that are between today and the closing date are displayed.

Ø Planned: Actions that start later than today appear

The status column in the list also indicates that the action is closed, current, or planned.

 

 

5.2.11.2. The action editor window

 

If you want to start a new action or edit an existing action, the action editor window will appear.

In the action window, you can edit the following data:

·        Action type: Choose here what the action should apply. These can be:

o   Product: if the action concerns a product. In this case, a product selection field will appear under the type of action, here you will have to specify the product.

o   Product group: if the action applies to a product group. In this case, besides the type of action, there is an option to inherit the action for the descendant groups and a product group selection field below which the product group to which the action applies is selected.

o   Warehouse: if the action applies to a warehouse. In this case, a store selection field will appear under the type of action where you need to specify the warehouse in which all the products in the action will be covered.

·        Current month button: The start of the action will be the first day of the current month, the end will be the last day of the month.

·        Next Month Button: The beginning of the action will be the first day of the following month, the end will be the last day of the following month.

·        Start of the action : the start date of the action.

·        End of action : The end of the action.

·        Discount: the amount of the action, in percentage. To the right, you will see a button pressed to display an auxiliary function that will help you calculate the percentage of the action based on the original price and promotional price you specify.

·        Price categories : Select the price categories for which you want the action to apply.

When you are done completing the data, you can save the action with the Save button, or you can use the Next button to record a new action immediately (by pressing the ENTER button on the discount field, the last action is done automatically).

 

5.2.12. HR, co-workers

 

In this module, you can manage staff who are also users of the software.

The module includes HR functions, here you can define the permissions of the staff.

 

5.2.12.1. List of employees
