If you enter the HR, staff module, you will see a list of employees.

The following high-priority filters are listed above the list:

Ø Starter Pager Filter: Use this filter to narrow down the list to the name of the selected letter.

Ø Only your own staff: if this option is enabled, you will only see your own staff in the list. If you turn it off, all partners' partners will be shown (partners can be recruited).

If you want to see more information about a colleague in the list, you can see the details bar by turning on the details mode . 

 

5.2.12.2. Collaboration panel for co-workers

 

If you are recording a new employee or editing a selected one, you will see the editor panel.

This is a complex module, so the data is on multiple pages. These pages are as follows:

/   Personal, basic data sheet

·        You can enter personal, basic data for this employee on this page, which are:

·        Full name: Full name of the employee. Required , but you do not need to enter your real or full name, as the software cannot verify this. This is not a login username! The user name will then be specified on the permissions tab.

·        Address: temporary or permanent address of the employee. It's not neccessary.

·        Telephone: contact information of the employee. It's not neccessary.

·        Email: the email address of the employee. It's not neccessary.

·        Date of birth: date of birth of the worker. It's not neccessary.

·        TAJ number: the social security number of the employee. It's not neccessary.

·        Tax ID: Employee Tax ID. It's not neccessary.

·        Company: Here you have to choose the company where the employee works. In the case of your own colleague, you have a "own company" here.

·        Location: if the company has more than one location, then the site where the employee performs the tasks must be selected.

·        Comments, additions: Anything else can be written and recorded here.

 

/   Profile picture

Use this tab to attach a profile image to the user.

Use the Browse button to find the file you want to attach, and the Delete button to delete the attachment.

This attached image will appear in the list details view before the employee's data.

 

/   Career, positions

Employee positions can be recorded on this page.

Steps to record a new position in steps:

1.     After pressingthe New button, the position editor panel appears.

2.     We must specify thedate of commencement of employment .

3.     Enter the end date ofthe employment relationship . If the employment relationship is of indefinite duration , this should be selected.

4.     Enter thename ofthe position and job. Next time this position name can be selected.

5. We     can specify the gross monthly salary of the employee.

6.     You can attach your work contract to the right with the […] browse button.

7.     Use the Save button to save the data that is then displayed in the list.

Use the Edit button to change the position of a member of the staff member, or press the Delete button to delete it from the list.

 

/   Cafeteria

If a colleague receives a fringe benefit, it can be recorded here.

How to fix a new cafeteria in steps:

1.     After pressingthe New button, the cafeteria editing panel appears.

2.     Enter the name of the allowance .

3.     Enter the first and last day of the allowance .

4.     You can record other descriptions and comments about the benefit.

5.     Use the Save button to save the data. The benefit will then appear in the list.

Use the Edit button to change the fringe benefit, and you can delete it from the list using the Delete button.

 

/   Privileges

In the case of our own staff, the eligibility settings must be completed.

These are:

·        User ID: this will be specified in the user name text box on the software login panel.

·        User Password: In the software login panel, you will need to enter this in the password text box to log in.

·        Administrator: grants administrator rights in the software.

·        Visitor: Limited access to software modules and functions.

·        Hide password: If you turn it off, your password will be visible.

Most of the page shows permissions per category and module. The software uses simplified privilege management on a statistical basis, so it is transparent for the first time who has rights. Here the function of each switch is clear.

There are two more switches below the authorization table:

·        You can change your bids on the invoice and in the merchandise: turn on if you don't want the user to overwrite the prices.

·        You can change permissions (including your own): this should be left on at least one user so that everyone does not exclude themselves from the system or some features, because then only the developer can help.

Below is the Assign Assignment Profile button. In the settings main menu, you can create authority profiles. These profiles can be copied to the authorization table.

 

/   Bank accounts

On this page we can record the bank accounts of the employee.

How to set up a new bank account in steps:

1.     After pressingthe New button, the bank account editing panel appears.

2.     Enter the bank name .

3. For a     foreign bank account, enter the SWIFT code .

4.     Enter the exact bank account number .

5. For a     foreign bank account, enter the IBAN . If the IBAN field is blank or contains invalid data, a yellow triangle with an exclamation mark (an audit algorithm running to the IBAN) is displayed after the IBAN.

6.     Use the Save button to save the data. The bank account will then appear in the list.

The Edit button allows you to change your bank account, and you can delete it from the list using the Delete button.

 

/   Events

Here you can record tasks and other events related to the user.

How to record a new event in steps:

1.     After pressingthe New button, the Event Editor panel appears.

2.     Enter the start and end of the event.

3.     When the job is completed or the event is closed, you can turn on the Executed / Closed switch.

4.     Enter the subject of the event.

5.     The description, comment is partly explained in the task or event.

6.     Use the Save button to save the data. The event will then appear in the list.

Use the Edit button to change the selected event in the list, and to delete it from the list, press Delete .

 

/   Documents

On this page, you can attach documents to your colleague.

Documents can be image files or document files.

On the left is a list of attached documents. The first column shows a small pictogram before the date of the files. If this is a yellow folder, the document remains in its original location and can be opened from there. If the pictogram is a gray cylinder, the document is saved in the database and will always be saved to the local computer when it is opened.

The preview image of the selected document is displayed on the right.

Buttons on the left-hand side of the list on the left-hand side, from left to right:

Ø Attach a new document: simple attachment, the document opens from its original location.

Ø Add and save a new document to the database: the document will be placed in the database (up to 5 MB) and the file will be saved to the local computer when opened.

Ø Delete Document Attachment: Deletes document attachment. If you have saved the document in the database, it will be deleted from it.

Ø Open a Document: Opens the document with the default program assigned to the document format (according to the operating system settings).

 

5.2.13. partner Groups

 

As with the products, the partners can be classified into different groups. The group will be selected on the partner data sheet.

These groups can be created in this module.

The partner group consists of one data, the group name.

When you add a new contact group or edit an existing group, the Partner Group Editor panel appears.

In this panel, we only need to enter one data: the name of the partner group.

When finished, the Save button saves the group.

 

5.2.14. CRM, partners

 

You can manage your partner data in this menu item.

 

5.2.14.1. List of partners

 

When you enter the module, a list of partners appears.

Above the list of partners, there is a first-page paging filter , which allows us to narrow the list to the names that begin with the selected letter.

 

5.2.14.2. The Partner Editor Panel

 

If you add a new partner or edit an existing one, the Partner Editor panel appears.

Partner data is divided into several tabs:

 

/   Basic data

On this page you can record the most important data of the partner, many of which are mandatory.

These data are:

·        Name: the full name of the partner, which includes the abbreviation of the company form (in case of a private contractor). Required data!

·        Email Address: The primary email address of the partner. The software sends the documents, payment reminders, alerts and other letters to this address.

·        Billing / mailing / shipping address: For all three types of addresses, the same data must be provided, with a name and tagged address information.

o    Billing address: the name and address where the partner's location is located will also be invoiced to this address. Mandatory for company!

o    Mailing address: the name and address to which you can send your partner's letters.

o    Shipping address: the name and address to which you can send parcels, parcels, shipments to the partner.

The two buttons on the right above the address data:

o   Use the first button to copy the billing address information to the mailing address data.

o   Use the second button to copy mailing address data to shipping address data.

·        Tax number: the partner's domestic tax number. It is not required for an individual or for a foreign company. Mandatory for domestic companies!

·        Community (EU) tax number: If the company has an EU tax number, it must be completed.

·        Group tax number: If the company is a member of a group of companies, the group tax number within the group must be entered here.

·        Group Corporate Taxpayer Identifier: If the company is a member of a group of companies, the taxpayer group ID must be entered here.

·        Partner type : type of partner, one of the following is mandatory:

o   Supplier

o   Buyer

o   Supplier and buyer

o   Own Company

o   Health Fund

o   Individual

o   Possible customer

o   Manufacturer

·        Partner Group : A group of partners. Data to be selected from the partner group strain .

·        Price category: Here you can choose a price category. When a partner sells something, the system will automatically offer the price for this price category.

·        Discount rate: if we give the partner an extra discount on our prices, then it should be entered here.

·        Default Payment Method: The preferred payment method for the partner. The customer will automatically be offered this on the partner account.

·        Default payment deadline: the payment deadline preferred by the partner, calculated in days. It is possible to agree with the partner in advance what payment deadline you are willing to pay and to enter here.

·        Credit line: the amount above which a partner is not allowed to sell anything.

·        Returns can be received from the partner: if we withdraw the goods from the partner, it can be switched on.

 

/   Contact, customer loyalty

You can enter additional contact and loyalty data on this page:

·        Master Purchaser: If our partner is a loyal customer, we can indicate this to the software by turning on this switch. The software can automatically make the partners a regular customer, and its options can be defined in program settings.

·        Loyalty card number: If the partner is a loyal customer and we have issued a loyalty card, you must enter the number here. If you enter or read the card number of the loyalty card in a document, sales card number field, you will automatically select the partner and receive the appropriate discounts on the items (loyalty discounts can be defined in the program settings).

·        Customer type : other type of customer. You can write anything here freely and you will be able to select it from the drop-down list next time.

·        The customer agrees to receive a newsletter: if the partner has agreed to receive a newsletter, it can be marked. Newsletters go to the e-mail address provided on the Basic Data tab. The software includes a newsletter module where newsletters can be compiled and campaigns launched.

·        Contact person (partner): the partner with whom we are in contact. The name can be written freely.

·        Dealer (own): our own salesperson who keeps in touch with the partner. You can choose from our own staff.

·        Phone Numbers: Write contact details for your partner here. If you enter more than one number, you must write each one in a new line. You can start a new line by simultaneously pressing the Shift + Enter buttons.

·        Notes: Write comments, information about the partner here. You can start a new line by simultaneously pressing the Shift + Enter buttons.

 

/   Locations

Here you can record the partner's locations. This is useful if the partner has more than one location and it is different to which one to request delivery.

The steps to record your location are:

1.     After pressingthe New button, the site editor panel will appear (this is the same as the one displayed when recording your own sites).

2.     Enter the partner's location ID .

3.     Fill in the name field.

4.     Enter the address of the partner's location .

5.     You can specify the telephone contact ofthe site.

6.     Use the Save button to save the data. The site will then appear in the list.

Use the Edit button to change the selected location in the list, and Delete to delete it from the list.

 

/   Profile picture / logo

Here we can attach a picture to the partner, which can be the partner's logo.

Use the Browse button to find and attach the appropriate image.

The Delete button to remove the coupling.

 

/   Other data

This page provides special CRM data for specific development needs.

 

/   Bank accounts

On this page we can record the partner bank accounts.

How to set up a new bank account in steps:

1.     After pressingthe New button, the bank account editing panel appears.

2.     Enter the bank name .

3. For a     foreign bank account, enter the SWIFT code .

4.     Enter the exact bank account number .

5. For a     foreign bank account, enter the IBAN . If the IBAN field is blank or contains invalid data, a yellow triangle with an exclamation mark (an audit algorithm running to the IBAN) is displayed after the IBAN.

6.     Use the Save button to save the data. The bank account will then appear in the list.

The Edit button allows you to change your bank account, and you can delete it from the list using the Delete button.

 

/   Documents

Use this tab to attach documents to your partner.

Documents can be image files or document files.

On the left is a list of attached documents. The first column shows a small pictogram before the date of the files. If this is a yellow folder, the document remains in its original location and can be opened from there. If the pictogram is a gray cylinder, the document is saved in the database and will always be saved to the local computer when it is opened.

The preview image of the selected document is displayed on the right.

Buttons on the left-hand side of the list on the left-hand side, from left to right:

Ø Attach a new document: simple attachment, the document opens from its original location.

Ø Add and save a new document to the database: the document will be placed in the database (up to 5 MB) and the file will be saved to the local computer when opened.

Ø Delete Document Attachment: Deletes document attachment. If you have saved the document in the database, it will be deleted from it.

Ø Open a Document: Opens the document with the default program assigned to the document format (according to the operating system settings).

 

/   Questionnaire data

In the program settings you can define a questionnaire for the partners.

On this page you can fill out this questionnaire.

The left column shows the questions.

You can write the answers in the right column.

 

/   Events

Here you can record tasks and other events related to the partner.

How to record a new event in steps:

1.     After pressingthe New button, the Event Editor panel appears.

2.     Enter the start and end of the event.

3.     When the job is completed or the event is closed, you can turn on the Executed / Closed switch.

4.     Enter the subject of the event.

5.     The description, comment is partly explained in the task or event.

6.     Use the Save button to save the data. The event will then appear in the list.

Use the Edit button to change the selected event in the list, and to delete it from the list, press Delete .

 

/   Processes

Module under development.

Here you will see a table of activities that can be associated with the partner, as a process that is combined with the same work numbers.

 

/   Partner staff

On this page, you can add staff to your partner.

When you add a new employee or edit a list in the list, you will see an editor panel for the staff (you can read it in the appropriate section).
The affiliate company will already be selected in the partner data sheet, do not change this setting (otherwise the employee will be transferred to another company).

Use the Break button to delete the selected partner member.

 

If we have completed the compilation of the contact and completed at least the mandatory data, then the Save Data button will save the contact.

 

5.3. Administration

                  

                  

5.3.0. Document management, document operation

 

This chapter describes how to manage your software. In the other chapters, this manual indicates only the differences and unique data.

The software can produce 32 types of documents.
Of which 23 vouchers are handled by a module, always configured for different documents:

·        Supplier side: invoice, delivery note, receipt (attachment), proforma (fee request), order, quotation.

·        Customer side: invoice, delivery note, receipt (attachment), manual invoice, auction, proforma (fee request), order, quotation.

·        Warranty management: warranty, warranty, warranty, warranty replacement, release for warranty, warranty inspection.

·        Warehouse site: inventory, warehouse entry, warehouse release.

This common module will now be presented.

Important information about documents:

 

v Document copying, inheritance:

An important feature of the documents is that one of them can be made with one of its items.
For example, we can make a delivery note from one supplier order, one invoice that is automatically closed and the receipt of the stock in stock is automatically completed:

Here, it is important to note that in the case of vouchers, the real inventory movement is carried out by warehouse receipts and warehouse release documents. These two vouchers are automatically generated upon completion of delivery notes , invoices, receipt attachments, and, if they are still unmanaged , in stock.

Copying and inheriting the documents will be discussed below.

 

v Pickup:

It is also possible to take items from multiple vouchers to a document.
For example, you can receive incoming items from multiple orders for an incoming delivery note or invoice:

The software records the amount of documents transferred to other vouchers and the amount that can be transferred.

Thus, a voucher and batch chain is created that can consist of more vouchers and items and usually ends with a warehouse operation.

Below is the module that can be used for picking.

 

v Document Serial Numbers:

It is very important to note that the documents run on a separate line number range per site and currency, ie in different virtual vouchers.

In program settings, you can set the serial number type for each document, leave it with default settings, or at least your customer account.

 

The structure and management of the voucher module will be explained below.

 

5.3.0.1. List of documents

 

When you enter a document module, you can see the list of documents. This list is similar for every document.

On the toolbar above the list, you can find the buttons you already know, but beyond the standard, there are additional features available here:

Ø NAV report: invoices issued to Hungarian partners for HUF 100,000 or above VAT must be reported to the tax administration's online account registration system. With this feature, you can manually start reporting unposted accounts or checking the status of accounts that have already been reported (the invoice report can be considered successful if your tax system has processed it). Customer accounts are reported individually after they are closed. If you do not succeed then you should use this feature. This button appears only in the Customer Accounts module.

It's easy to use, just press the button, the software starts to work and when you are done, you can see which documents you have succeeded or failed to report.

Ø Equalization: the button is displayed in the customer and supplier account module. If you choose an account, you can use this feature to quickly sign up for your account and cash flows (bank, cashier) to see how much you have been billed for.

Here, all the necessary data (which we check) will be filled out by default, and only the amount to be settled will be entered and the balance will be closed with the Save button.

Ø Bank Statement: Also appears only in Customer and Supplier Account Module. It allows you to process bank extract files automatically so that your invoices are automatically settled. If we ask our customers to include the exact invoice number in the note when making the transfer, the software will recognize from the bank statement which invoice the customer has settled.

First, select the bank (currently the software can process the extract file of three banks), then browse the extract file, and then use the Scan button to perform the processing.

You can also see a Loan Verification button here , which displays the Loan Management module (this module will be discussed later in this manual).

There is also the option to turn on details mode for your documents , with the following tabs (may expand later): 

¤ Document Items: You can see the details of items recorded on the voucher.

¤ Balance: a list of voucher settlements with the serial number, execution time, amount, currency of the bank or cashier cash flow document.

¤ Employee Data: Details of the employee who issued the document are displayed on the tab.

 

Below the toolbar are the highlighted filters and list settings that are:

Ø Year: The list shows only the documents issued in the selected year.

Ø Month: Only the documents displayed in the selected month are shown in the list. Here you can choose to show the vouchers for each month.

Ø Currency: filtering for the currency of documents.

Ø Location: Filtering for the site where the vouchers are set. Here are all site settings to show all of your site certificates at once.

Ø List of items: this is a special way of listing documents. If you turn it on, the list will show the items in the list, grouped according to each document. This allows us to create additional useful individual statements.

Ø Quick filter: this allows you to narrow the entire list to the text or data you enter. To help you find your data quickly.

 

5.3.0.2. The document editor window

 

When you create a new document, the document editor window appears with the name and serial number of the document at the top.
New documents that have not been closed yet have only one temporary serial number. Receipts will only receive a final serial number at the moment of their closure.

The picture shows the customer account document editor:

 

The document window consists of two parts. In the upper section, you can edit the details of the document, the items in the lower part, or change the financial balance of the document or attach documents to it.

 

Above the tabs on the top, three switches to the right are related to the nature and management of the document:

þ Accounts Receivable: If we have agreed with the partner that the executing party will invoice more than one successive at a time, then we can create a summary account (the items can be grouped with the item group). If you create such a bill, you must activate this switch.

þ Self-billing: The invoice issuance may be issued by the buyer of the products or by the recipient of the services. In this case, this switch must be activated.

þ Email: If this option is active, the document is not only printed but also made into a PDF file that the software sends to the email address of the partner.

þ Archiv: If you want to exclude a document from the official framework (ie not used for any official transaction), you must activate this option.

 

There are four switches to the right of the items in the document that are related to managing the items:

þ KT / CSK Fees: Displays environmental product charges on the voucher (invoice). When we turn on, which products we have given environmental product charges will be included in the summary of product fees and invoices when commenting on their items.

þ Inventory Movement: Document items carry stock movements. If it is active on a voucher, it will automatically generate a warehouse receipt or warehouse release when it is closed (because these last two documents carry the real set of moves).

þ Reverse VAT: if the customer pays the VAT on the goods and services listed on the invoice, ie there are reverse VAT items on the voucher, this option must be enabled.

þ VAT-free: If you want to issue an invoice without VAT (or we have received it), we need to activate this option. If you turn it on, the VAT key for all items will be 0%, if you turn it off, the VAT key will match the items again.

 

 

Document details are on three tabs:

/   Header data

Here you can enter the following data, depending on the type of document in question:

·        Inventory Type: Only inventory can be selected by inventory method:

o   Part of warehouse: if you want to inventory only part of your warehouse stock. Only the quantities of the inventories will be corrected.

o   Whole warehouse: if you want to inventory the entire warehouse. In this case, the quantities of non-inventory items will be written to zero in the inventory.

o   Opening kit: If you add a new warehouse, you need to add an opening kit. Its operation is similar to the inventory of the entire warehouse.

·        Inventory on multiple machines: Optional on inventory only . If you want to count on more than one computer, you need to select it. When checked, the inventory voucher appears in the inventory list to open on other computers and add items.

·        Alien Serial Number: Only data that can be specified on supplier documents . Here you can enter the original serial number of the received receipt.

·        Order number: Here you can enter the serial number of the other order document related to the document.

·        Special ID: can only be specified on supplier documents . Space reserved for arbitrary identification data.

·        Status: the status of the document (unlocked, closed, completed, etc.).

·        Partner: this is where the partner can be selected (which appears on the voucher, mandatory data ). You can enter a loyalty card or master card number in the text card number text field (or read it with a barcode reader) in front of the partner selection field, so the software automatically selects the contact associated with the number.

·        Partner Delivery Address: The one that appears on the document is required to select the address to which the products on the document will be delivered.

·        Partner bank account: appears on supplier vouchers. If the incoming voucher is an invoice and a payment method is set, then you must select it.

·        Date: the date of issue of the document.

·        Performance: the time the services are displayed on the voucher, or the date on which the items on the items were delivered.

·        Method of payment: means of payment for the voucher (cash, bank transfer, bank card, receipt, etc.). On which voucher this data is displayed, it is mandatory to fill in this data.

·        Payment deadline: the deadline for the financial execution of the document.

·        Currency: the currency in which the items on the voucher are counted.

·        Currency: if the currency is different from the default currency of the system (which is currently Ft), then the daily exchange rate must be entered here if it is not loaded automatically. In the default currency of the system (which is now Ft), it must always be 1.

·        My bank account: appears only on customer receipts . We need to select the bank account numbers of our own company for which we are waiting for the transfer of the partner. In the case of a cash account, the software also requests this information in case the customer still settles some of the amount by bank transfer.

·        Cashier: alsoappears on customer receipts only . We have to select the cashier into which the buyer will pay the amount in the case of a cash account. It is also advisable to select a transfer account, as it may happen that the buyer pays the money in cash.

·        Warehouse: The warehouse to which the product moves recorded on the document are linked. Required data. It should also be specified if there is only a service on the invoice because it may occur when the document is derived or corrected, that products are still added to it.

·        Work Count: Work Numbers Accompany Documents and Activities in Other Modules to Workflow. The field can be dropped and the appropriate work number can be selected or a new work number can be generated with the button to the right of the field. Not mandatory.

 

/   More details

This page contains only a few additional data that can be filled out if necessary:

·        Partner country: the country of a foreign partner can be selected from a country list where we deliver the items on the document.

·        Freight Volume: The way to share costs. Enter the code for the correct freight rate here. Freely editable field, the data you enter will be selectable later.

·        Mode of transport : method of delivery. You can freely enter any information that will be available next time.

·        Financial representative: if an intermediary financial agent complicates the transaction between us and the partner specified on the voucher, you must select the financial representative here from the partner body (where you must be previously registered).

 

/   Comments

Here you can write four notes on the document (the default comments can be edited in the program settings):

·        Basic note: this note will appear on any printed or saved document in PDF.

·        Remittance: this note will only appear on the printed document or saved in PDF if it is a payment method.

·        Legal notice: this note also appears only in the case of a transfer, and we may warn the partner here about actions and other legal obligations due to late payment.

·        Other: the text that you write here appears after the comments.

 

The lower part of the window also occupies three tabs:

/   Items

This page is from the list of items and the associated toolbar.

The buttons on the toolbar are:

Ø New Item: Add a new item to the list. The batch editor window appears, in which all the data in the document batch can be edited. (The item editor is discussed below in this manual.)

Ø Edit: open the selected item in the list for editing. The editor window appears, in which you can modify the item details.

Ø Delete Item: delete the selected item. It is also possible to delete multiple items quickly without question by repeatedly pressingthe Shift + Del buttons.

Ø Item Receipt : A module is displayed that allows you to transfer items from multiple other documents to the current document (picking). (This manual is discussed below in this manual).

Ø Fast editing mode: if you turn on quick editing mode, the table changes and you can record or edit items directly in the table, all data can be edited. If the quick editing mode is off, only the quantities can be modified directly in the table.

In fast editing mode, you can start recording the first item with the small New Item button in the upper left corner of the table (this is not the same as the large New Item button), then enter the data from column to column by pressing ENTER . When you reach the end of the line, you will automatically receive a new line. The last line that you missed will be automatically deleted when you save or close the document.

Ø Search: A search window will appear that lets you search for the text or number you entered in the item list:

Ø Data collector: if a data collector is configured, you can retrieve the collected item data (the serial numbers, batch numbers generate the item list).

Ø Assemble: based on the selected item, a new production worksheet is opened.

Ø Disassemble: based on the selected item, a new dismantling worksheet opens.

Ø Batch Import: You can import an item list from an excel file (the format is fixed if you hold the mouse pointer over the button, this format is shown).

Ø Discount: All items can be discounted at the same time with this feature:

Ø Warehouse Reset: Displays only on an Inventory voucher, and immediately resets the entire stock of the selected warehouse. Double after confirmation.

Ø List Generation: At the moment, it only appears on the inventory voucher, with the help of which you can put all the items to be listed in the list with two clicks, so that only the quantities have to be changed. It significantly speeds up the inventory process.
First you need to choose which product group products should be included in the list (you can specify whether you want to list the products of your descendant groups), then you can set whether to list only the items in stock and whether the amount of items to be displayed should be zero (if no, then the quantities show the current stock level, this is recommended).
Finally, after you press the Create Item List button, the list is created in which you only need to modify the quantities during inventory.

Ø Order by Partner: A vendor's order is displayed on a voucher, all inventory deficient products (below the minimum inventory level) are included in the list that can be ordered from a partner selected in the document header (the default supplier can be set on the product data sheet).

Ø Warehouse order: also a supplier order is displayed on a voucher, all inventory shortages (below the minimum inventory level) associated with the selected warehouse are included in the list.

 

Under the item toolbar, a bar is displayed, and the features listed here are:

§ Code  Reading: This field allows you to read the barcodes of the products with a barcode reader that are automatically added to the item list. If a product is already in the list, its quantity will increase by one. If the product for the scanned code is not found, the field will turn red for one second.

§  Unit price net / gross: this switch is displayed in quick editing mode and can be set to show and see the net or gross unit price of the products directly in the table.

§  Fast filtering: only those rows that contain the text you type in the quick filter or other data will appear in the list.

 

Below this is the item list. The columns in the list can be rearranged, resized, but the order of the items is linked to the documents (as recorded), so we cannot sort them in order of our own.
The item list can operate in two modes: normal and the fast editing mode already described.

 

/   Balance

It was about settling the vouchers. On this page, you can view the voucher balancing items. Here you can add new payouts or re-write one before:

With the New Item button, you can add a new equalization to the voucher, edit with the Edit button to change the selected balance on the list, and the Delete button to delete the equalization.

 

/   Documents

On this page, you can attach files to the document.

Documents can be image files or document files.

On the left is a list of attached documents. The first column shows a small pictogram before the date of the files. If this is a yellow folder, the document remains in its original location and can be opened from there. If the pictogram is a gray cylinder, the document is saved in the database and will always be saved to the local computer when it is opened.

The preview image of the selected document is displayed on the right.

Buttons on the left-hand side of the list on the left-hand side, from left to right:

Ø Attach a new document: simple attachment, the document opens from its original location.

Ø Add and save a new document to the database: the document will be placed in the database (up to 5 MB) and the file will be saved to the local computer when opened.

Ø Delete Document Attachment: Deletes document attachment. If you have saved the document in the database, it will be deleted from it.

Ø Open a Document: Opens the document with the default program assigned to the document format (according to the operating system settings).

 

At the bottom of the document window, you'll find:

·        Exhibited by: the self-employed person who made and closed the document. It will also appear on the printed (or saved to PDF) document.

·        Net: the total net value of items on the voucher.

·        VAT: the total value of VAT on the items on the voucher.

·        Gross: the gross value of the items in the vouchers.

Ø Save and Exit: Use this button to save the document without closing and close the document window. So we can put the document back for later editing.

Ø Save: Use this button to save the current document details without leaving the document.

Ø New document based on this: we can create another document from the document. For example, one invoice from one delivery note can be used to make an invoice with the header and item details matched. There will be a relationship between the original and the new document and their items (unless we disconnect the link). The new document will be editable. This function works only with closed documents.

To use:
1. First you need to select the type of document you want to make from the current document.

2. You can set whether to attach the new document to this document (you should leave the connection unless you want a smooth copy).

3. You can select whether all items should be transferred to the new document or only those that have not yet been transferred to another document if the documents are connected.

4. Finally, create a new document with the Create Document button.

5. The current document closes and the new one opens, which can be edited.

Ø Cancel: We can cancel an already closed document. The reversed voucher will match the new one, with the difference that the quantities of the items will be opposite. In the event of reversal, it is possible to reverse the stock movement of the products. The cancellation voucher is immediately closed, there is no way to edit it.

Ø Correct: We can correct an already closed document. Then a new document will be created, listing the original items with the opposite sign, the original items below them, but with the original quantities. When the correction document is created, it is still possible to edit it.

Ø Print: Print the document or save it as a PDF file.

Ø Preview: view the print image of the document without printing.

Ø Closing a document / completing a document: we can close the still unopened document. This will give the document a new serial number. In the case of a customer account, if it is to be reported, the data will be provided to the tax authority. When you move a set of documents, a warehouse entry or a warehouse release document is made automatically (because they carry a real stock move).
If you have opened an already closed document, you can use this button to mark the document as completed. If the voucher is an invoice, it will be automatically marked as completed if the total amount of the invoice is settled.

 

 

5.3.0.3. Edit document items

 

If you want to add a new item to the list using the New Item button on the toolbar above the list of items, or to modify any of the selected items with the Edit button, the item editor window appears.

The top line of the window shows what type of document is being edited, followed by the serial number of the document.

Below this is an information bar about the selected product, from which you can read the unit price of the product, the stock level, how much it is booked (in order), how much can be sold or can be returned.

You can then enter the following information:

Ø Barcode finder: if you are scanning with a barcode reader or manually entering the barcode of the product (which is acknowledged with the ENTER key when manually entered), the product corresponding to the barcode is automatically selected, then the cursor immediately jumps to the pricing table.

Ø Product Group: Here you can filter the list of product selections below for the specified product group.

þ Derived Groups: If you turn it on, products in the subgroups of the group set in the product group filter will also appear in the product selector.

Ø Product code: product selection based on product code.

Ø Item number: product selection by article number.

Ø Name: Select the product by its name.

þ Only in stock: if active, only products from the selected warehouse can be selected.

Ø Price category: we can choose the price for the selected product. By default, you will see the price category that belongs to your document partner. After the price category, there is a button that you can press to edit the price categories of the selected product.

Ø Unit price net / gross: Use this option to select whether the pricing table should be the net or gross unit price of the product.

Ø Price table: Here you can edit the item's price and quantity data and calculate the calculated values ​​here.

The following columns and options are available here:

o   Unit price: the unit price of the product. By default, the price for the price category specified by the partner is offered here by the software that we can modify.

o   Quantity: quantity of product. By default, you get 1 here, which can be rewritten to the desired amount. It can also be a broken value. To write a negative amount here, first enter the amount and then press the minus [-] key on the keyboard.

o   Unit: Quantity of the product. The default quantity unit set for the product is displayed here, and we can overwrite it if necessary.

o   Net value: calculated data, product of product and quantity.

o   VAT%: VAT rate set for the product. Cannot be modified to avoid accidental errors. If the voucher is free of VAT, then 0 will be displayed here if the reverse tax or a batch is free, it is also 0.

o   Amount of VAT: calculated data, gross value minus net value.

o   Gross value: calculated data, multiplying the net value by the VAT key.

o   Discount: In this line you can give a discount on the unit price of the product. By default, the software fills in if the partner qualifies for a discount, and can then be modified.
The discounted values ​​are shown to the right of the discount.

Ø Item group: used for a general account (previously mentioned), items will be grouped in the invoice if the same item item data is entered. In the case of a correction invoice, there will be two fixed text items, "Original Items" and "Modified Items".

Ø Cost center: If the item is eligible as an expense, you can choose a cost center (which comes from the cost center).

Ø Warehouse: the warehouse from which the specified quantity is withdrawn or added to the product. By default, the voucher will be stored here as well.

Ø Storage: The storage space within the warehouse that is reserved for the product.

 

You can enter additional information on the following tabs (tabs):

/   Comments

You can comment on the item. The comment will appear on the printed (or saved to PDF) document directly under the item.

 

/   Batch numbers, factory numbers, sizes

Here you can specify which batch (or LOT, batch) numbers of the item selected on the item, their unique serial numbers (the amount of which corresponds to the quantity of the product) and the individual size data (the dimensions can be defined on the product data sheet).

The individual data come under each other.

In the case of a batch, a batch number can be selected from a list.

Upon receipt, the specified batch number will take the products (in this case a batch number must be provided) and the serial numbers will be included in the batch.

When released, the software will deliver the batch number of items specified by the software by first extracting the specified number of products from the factory.
If a batch number or serial number is not found in the release at release, it is ignored and applied to the set inventory rule (such as FIFO).

 

/   Product fees

A list of environmental product charges for the selected product and their item quantity. Only for viewing.

 

/   Warranty, warranty, condition

The date of manufacture of the products on the item, or the start of warranty and the expiry date of its warranty or guarantee may be specified.

You can also set the status of the products:

o   Used

o   Demolished

o   Damaged

Below you can freely describe the injuries and aesthetic defects found on the products.

 

/   Service data

If you opened the item editor from a service module, you can enter the following data on this page:

o   Colleague: The employee who is involved in repairing the product should be set up.

o   Work time: set the start and end of service work.

o   Ready: When the product is repaired, you can turn it on.

o   Billing: If the item is billed, turn it on.

o   Received accessories: you can freely describe the accessories you have received with the product (for example, power cord, power supply, etc.).

o   Fault description: we can freely describe the error produced by the product.

 

/   Accessories products

A list of accessories for this product is available here.

If you double-click an accessory, or right-click, a small pop-up menu will appear, one of which will add the accessory to the list of documents, and the other will open the accessory product sheet for editing.

 

/   Substitutes

List of replacement products for this product. Substitutes are products that we can offer to the buyer if they are not in stock from the originally selected product.

If you double-click an accessory, or right-click, a small pop-up menu will appear, one of which will add the accessory to the list of documents, and the other will open the accessory product sheet for editing.

 

At the bottom of the item window you will find:

Ø Label Print Switch: When enabled, the label automatically saves as many labels as the item quantity when saving the item.

Ø Continuous Recording Switch: If enabled, if you move forward with the ENTER button between fields, pressing the ENTER button automatically saves and returns a new blank item data sheet.

Ø Next Item Number Switch: If enabled and continuous recording, then the next item with item number will be selected on the next new item data sheet.

Ø Work Count: The work count combines different documents and activities as a process. By default, the job number set on the document is here.

Ø Cancel button: If you do not want to save the item, use this button to close the item editor.

Ø Reset button: resets the item editor, all data fields will be completely blank when a new entry is recorded, and the original item data will be restored when editing an item.

Ø Label Printing button: The label printer window appears, where you can print the right amount of labels for your products.

Ø Catalog button: The price catalog is displayed (previously mentioned, this is a special module for assembling individually configurable products).

Ø Next button: this button appears only when a new item is recorded. Saves the current item and adds a new blank item edit data sheet.

Ø Save button: save the item and then close the item editor.

 

 

5.3.0.4. Receiving items from other vouchers (picking)

 

If you press the batch transfer button on the toolbar above the items in the document editor, the Transmission of Document Items window appears. 

This window consists of three parts:

· You        can select the details of the documents whose headers and items are to be listed.

Here you can enter the following information:

o   Document type: the type of document for which we want to take items.

o   Partner: the partner that is on the documents that we would take items from.

o   Period: the period of the exhibition of the documents.

o   Currency: the currency of the vouchers.

o   Payment method: means of payment of documents.

o   It also lists a negligible amount of items: items in the list that would not, in principle, have a quantity that can be transferred.

·        Below is a list of voucher headers, from which you select a header for the document, the items that appear below it.

If you click on a document in the first column by clicking on it, all its items will be marked with their maximum transferable quantities.

There are two buttons above the list of document headers, one can be used to select all the documents with a single click (with each item), the second button can be used to uncheck each document (as well as their items).

·        The bottom section shows a list of items in the header of the selected document.

Here, in the first column, you can switch the item to be handed over or not. In the last quantity to be transferred, you can write the quantity you want to transfer. This should not be more than free volume.

The two buttons above the item list, the first of which selects all items for transfer, and the second selects all items from the list.

If you have selected all the receipts and items you want to transfer, you can use the Receive Items button to launch the item.

Then the item takeover window disappears and the items received will be in the items in our document.

If you change the quantities on the received items, the transferable amount will also be changed on the original documents because the entire item chain is recalculated.

 

5.3.1. Sale

 

The functionality of the vouchers in this module is described in Section 5.3.0. Document management, the operation of vouchers has already been presented in the chapter.

The manual now covers only other information for each document type.

 

5.3.1.1. Invoice

 

The invoice is an official document certifying the delivery of the products, the performance of the services, and plays an important role in tax accounting.

There are basically three types, the standard , the cancellation (storno) and the correction invoice (their preparation and handling was discussed in chapter 5.3.0).

It is important to include two concepts related to invoices:

Advance

No separate down payment account exists. If you want to deposit an advance on an account, you must add it as an item to the document item list. To do this, you must register an “Advance” product called “Down payment” in the product body and select it as a new item in an invoice, then specify the amount of the advance as the amount of the deposit and 1 for the amount.

On the final account, which is the counterpart of the prepayment account, the same prepayment item must be added as the item, with the amount of the pre-specified advance, but this time to give the value -1.

Return

There are several practices to take back the products.

One is to "mince" the returned product on a normal customer account, when the buyer purchases another, more expensive product and, in the price of it, approves the value of the returned product.

The other practice is to cancel the original invoice and then return the product on a supplier delivery note or receipt of the warehouse.

 

5.3.1.2. Delivery Note

 

Delivery note A voucher certifying the location of the goods and the fulfillment of the order, which the supplier issues when delivering one or more products to another location (eg partner, other site, etc.).

The delivery note can also be canceled. In this case, the opposite of the previous stock moves can be done (the returned products are returned to stock).

 

5.3.1.3. Received (attachment)

 

While the invoice  refers to the execution of the  transaction , the receipt confirms receipt of the consideration. In this software, the receipt is only included as an appendix for software tracking of cash register sales and product movements.

Upon request, we can link the software to the cash register with the appropriate documentation. In this case, the software generates receipts based on the data received from the cash register.

 

5.3.1.4. Manual invoice

 

It is also a supporting document for the accounting of the invoices of the manual invoice block.

 

5.3.1.5. Seeding Rate

 

Special voucher, which is a mixture of betrayal and delivery note. This means that it serves as a quote, but you can also move a stock (warehouse in and out through vouchers).

 

5.3.1.6. Proforma (fee request)

 

Proforma or prize money is used if you want to ask for the amount to be included in the invoice before the performance (delivery of goods, services). If the payment is made by the customer, we can execute and issue the invoice.

 

5.3.1.7. Order

 

Purchase orders can be recorded and delivery orders or invoices can be issued on delivery of the products or services.

If we operate a webshop, the orders from it will appear in this module as a customer order.

 

5.3.1.8. Quotes

 

A simple quote on products and services that a customer accepts can make other vouchers, such as an invoice, from this offer.

 

5.3.2. Acquisition

 

The functionality of the vouchers in this module is described in Section 5.3.0. Document management and the operation of the vouchers have also been presented in the chapter.

The manual now covers only other information for each document type.

 

5.3.2.1. Invoice

 

The invoice is an official document certifying the delivery of the products, the performance of the services, and plays an important role in tax accounting.

There are basically three types, the standard , the cancellation (storno) and the correction invoice (their preparation and handling was discussed in chapter 5.3.0).

 

5.3.2.2. Delivery Note

 

Delivery note A voucher certifying the location of the goods and the fulfillment of the order, which the supplier issues when delivering one or more products to another location (eg partner, other site, etc.).

The delivery note can also be canceled. In this case, the opposite of the previous stock moves can be done (the returned products are returned to stock).

 

5.3.2.3. Receipt

 

If we receive a receipt from our partner, we can record this document with the type.

 

5.3.2.4. Proforma (fee request)

 

Proforma or prize money is used if you want our partner to ask us for the amount to be included in the invoice before delivery (delivery of goods, services). If we make a payment to our partner, you will be billed to us. At this point, we only need to convert the biller to an invoice.

 

5.3.2.5. Order

 

It is used to record our order requests for suppliers. Upon receipt of the products or the performance of the services, we may issue a delivery note or invoice based on these.

 

5.3.2.6. Quotes

 

A quote from a supplier for products or services that we accept may make other vouchers, such as an invoice, from this offer.

 

5.3.3. warehouse movements

 

In this module there is a mix of 5.3.0. Document management, vouchers described in the operation of vouchers (inventory, warehouse entry and warehouse vouchers) and new ones for which only the editor of the item is the same as that of 5.3.0. (item inter-warehouse delivery, inter-warehouse order, scrapping). For the latter, the management of the module is described.

5.3.3.1. Intermediate Product Transfer

 

The editor of this module is different from the traditional document, but the editor of the item is the same.

If you start a new warehouse interchange, its editor window appears.

The structure of this editor is similar to that of other documents, there are header data and there is a list of items that we can edit in the same way.

The following information must be provided in the header:

·        Dated: the date the document was made.

·        Leave items:

o   Immediate automatic batch receipt when the document is closed

o   Manual batch receipt, in which case we must specify the expected date of receipt. This is to be used if the document has already been prepared in advance and we want to carry out the transfer of the products with the software if the physical is actually done. The receiving person must enter the quantities received in the transferred column, enter the date of receipt and then close the document.

·        Original Warehouse: The warehouse from which the products are transferred to the destination warehouse.

·        Target Warehouse: The warehouse where we store the products from the original warehouse.

·        Handed over: our colleague who released the products from the original warehouse.

·        Received: the employee who took over the products in the target warehouse.

There is also a quick editing mode which, when turned on, allows you to edit all data of the items directly in the list. Turning this off can only change the amount received in the transferred column.

It is also possible to attach documents, as described for other modules.

If you close the Intermediate Transfer Certificate with the Lock Document button , you can print a list of products to be handed over. If the receipt of the items is set to manual receipt, then later, when the products arrive at the warehouse, we will have to open this document again, and enter the quantities received in the transferred column, then

 

 

5.3.3.2. Intermediate order

 

Same as 5.3.3.1. Intermediate product delivery with the module discussed at the point, except that it is only ordering products from another warehouse, no stock movement.

First, choose which warehouse you want to order the products from.

Then record the items to be ordered in the item list.

Finally, close the document.

Later, we can open the same Intermediate Order Certificate and the Intermediate Transfer Document Generation button that appears then creates an inter-warehouse handover document that can be modified before it is closed.

 

5.3.3.3. stock-taking

 

The inventory process is as follows:

1. In the Administration main menu, select the Storage Movements module.

2.To switch to Inventory sub-module.

3. Recording a new inventory.

4.To set the Inventory Type setting, which has already been described in the General Description section of the Certificate Module.

5.Select the warehouse to which you want to add the opening kit.

6.There are several possibilities to carry out the inventory:
    6.1. To add items to the empty list one by one, there are two ways:
        6.1.1. The item editor that opens the New Item button (the item editor has a Next button to quickly record items in a row).
        6.1.2. The Fast Food. (quick edit) button to activate the quick editing mode where items can be recorded (and edited) directly in the list.
    6.2. Another, more convenient solution for inventory inventory creation is to create a list of commodity listings in the blank list from the base list . (list generation) button (on the toolbar).

7. As soon as the list appears (at the bottom), you can go to the top of the scroll bar and then click on How much. (quantity) column to edit the quantities continuously. After entering the quantity of each product and then pressing the ENTER button, you always jump to the next line, you can enter the quantity of the product, then ENTER again and so on.

When the inventory is completed, the Print button can be used to check the Inventory Difference List (printed by the module and after the inventory is closed).

If everything is okay, you can close the Inventory button with the Document Lock button. The following will happen automatically: Inventory difference list (printable or closed) will be displayed , then a Warehouse Input voucher will be created to replace the missing quantities (in case of opening, the quantity of this item will match the inventory quantities), then a Warehouse release will be createdvoucher to describe the surplus quantities from the stock (this is only if there is a product from which the inventory is less than the original stock, this will not appear in the opening case). Warehouse entry or warehouse release documents can be printed, or, if not required, just close the print preview window for these documents.
A warehouse release or a warehouse receipt is created because only these vouchers modify the inventory (so these vouchers are generated, for example, inbound or outbound invoices, even in the case of delivery notes).
Finally, the inventory window closes and the inventory that is now closed will be shown in the inventory list. At that time, the products will be in stock with the right quantities (a batch is created for each product) that can be checked under the Master Data / Stocks .

 

 

5.3.3.4. Warehouse input

 

The stock is taken over by the warehouse's receipt. It works the same as 5.3.0. Document management, as described in the operation of vouchers .

 

 

5.3.3.5. Warehouse release

 

The release of the products from the stock is done by the warehouse release document. It works the same as 5.3.0. Document management, as described in the operation of vouchers .

 

 

5.3.3.6. scrapping

 

Disposal operation is the same as in 5.3.3.1. Intermediate transfer is described in section, except that here you can only select a warehouse with a type of warehouse that is a scrap warehouse.

 

                                     

6. Tax authority control reporting

                                     

Tax Authority Data Provisioning under the Settings, Data Management Main Menu Group, Tax Authorities. data provider. starts with icon.
Other questions and answers related to tax authority information can be found here.
